summary: Plan a launch campaign with channel messaging, SEO support, and execution checkpoints before handoff.

inputs.name: offer_context
inputs.description: Product, feature, or event details to promote.

outputs.name: campaign_brief
outputs.description: Core strategy, positioning, and audience segments.

outputs.name: seo_support_notes
outputs.description: Search intent, landing-page keywords, and metadata suggestions.

budget.notes: Keep source intake narrow and focus on launch-ready outputs.

gates.rule: Confirm the request is campaign planning rather than sales enablement or product strategy.
Require clear audience, offer, and timeline before generating channel tactics.
Return one brief with consistent message, channel, and measurement sections.

steps.action: Define campaign objective, audience, success metric, and launch window.
Map content formats, publishing cadence, and CTA coverage across priority channels.
Add keyword targets, search-intent clusters, and landing-page metadata guidance.
Resolve conflicts and produce the final campaign brief with execution checkpoints.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read launch notes, brand inputs, and existing collateral.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Draft campaign artifacts when the caller requests a file output.

verification.method: Check the brief names audience, offer, and primary KPI.
Check each chosen channel has message, CTA, and publish timing.
Check the deliverable contains keyword and landing-page guidance.

success_criteria: A single launch brief covers audience, narrative, channels, and measurement.
Content and SEO recommendations align with the same offer and timeline.
No unsupported claims or missing CTA paths remain in the final handoff.

rollback.when: If the brief mixes conflicting audiences, channels, or metrics, revert to the scoped launch summary and regenerate downstream steps.
